summary: Phase 39 Terminology Audit fully validated. All backend (14 pytest cases) and frontend (P0+P1+P2) flows pass. AI fix uses canonical term, Apply persists override and sets status=fixed, Discover proposes new clusters (7 proposals received), RBAC blocks clients on all /admin/qa/term-audit/* endpoints, 5 seed clusters auto-seeded and visible.

issues: When a row is marked 'fixed' via Apply, it disappears from the list because the default filter is 'open'. The 'fixed' status badge can only be confirmed by switching the filter dropdown to 'fixed' or 'all'. This is acceptable UX but worth noting — alternatively show a transient success toast naming the now-fixed row.

critical_code_review_comments: AdminQAPlaybook.jsx is 1242 lines — getting large; consider splitting TerminologyAuditCard, ContentAuditCard, ReleaseGateCard into separate files for maintainability.
qa_terminology_audit.py concentrates seed data + scanner + AI logic in one module — fine for now but split into scanner/ai/storage submodules if it grows further.
All endpoints correctly use require_role('admin'); RBAC validated.
seed_data_creation: Test created and cleaned up: 6 inconsistencies (via scan), 1 doc_override (via apply), 1 ad-hoc test cluster (added then removed). 5 seed clusters in db.term_clusters preserved as required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 39 tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_phase39_term_audit.py. They are self-contained: scan creates inconsistencies, lifecycle test picks the first to AI-fix → apply → cleanup. Test cluster gets removed via Motor in fixture teardown. If running again, run the post-test cleanup script (deletes status in open/dismissed/fixed and overrides with source_term_inconsistency_id) before re-running browser-based 'fixed-row-disappears' check.
validated_endpoints: RBAC: client gets 401/403 on all 4 sampled endpoints
rca of the issue: No defects found. All assertions on Phase 39 spec pass. AI rewrite confirmed to strip non-canonical variants. Override persisted and inconsistency transitions to status='fixed' in DB.
